steps.title: Set the equations equal
steps.content:
Both equations are solved for $y$. Since they both equal the same variable, set the right sides equal to each other:

$$-\frac{1}{9}x = \frac{1}{2}x$$

steps.title: Solve for x
steps.content:
Collect all $x$ terms on the left side:

$$-\frac{1}{9}x - \frac{1}{2}x = 0$$

Convert to common denominator 18:

$$-\frac{2}{18}x - \frac{9}{18}x = 0$$

$$-\frac{11}{18}x = 0$$

$$x = 0$$

That matches choice C — C is correct.
